title: U.S. Dollar Rallies as Investors Seek Safety
summary: The dollar strengthened as the widening Middle East conflict set off a flight to safety in global markets.    The WSJ Dollar Index was up 0.5% after touching a five-week high earlier in the session. The currency's strength was driven by demand for so-called safe-haven assets, plus expectations that an oil-price spike could lead the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates higher.
